arm_a.covered_cells: FUN + casual-R1 wipe: ALL 52 cells exact; casual-R1 E[net incl. comp]: all 52 cells exact; farmer EVs: D1 all cells exact, D2 sign-exact (exact value at sigma = t), D3 T1 cells exact; baseline T1 grinder P_double: exact capped DP; D3 T1 stopper P(tau): exact
arm_a.d3_stopper_dp: exact exit-state distribution of every T1 stopper variant per t via banded exact-Fraction Gaussian elimination on the absorbing chain (states phi+1..tau-1, steps 2W - b', W ~ Bin(b', (1-t)/2), b' = min(b, bank)); EV per rho and P(reach tau) derive from the exits; the b = 1 variants are independently cross-checked against the two-boundary gambler's-ruin closed form (exact equality); T2 stopper EVs are MC-only (the proposal pins the DP 'on T1'), with the T1 DP anchoring the shared kernel
arm_a.fun_T1: P_ahead_ref(T1, t, design, size) exact via math.comb binomial: W ~ Bin(100, (1-t)/2); baseline/D3 ahead iff W >= 51; D1/D2 ahead iff 2W - 100 + 100*sigma > 0 i.e. W > 50 - 50*sigma (the proposal's own threshold) - W >= 50/48/46/41 for sigma = 0.02/0.05/0.10/0.20
arm_a.fun_T2: exact integer-support DP convolution of the single-ticket round net (units in {+7, +2, 0, -1}) over 100 R1 steps; ahead iff s > -100*sigma (baseline/D3: s > 0)
identities_asserted: D1-FUN == D2-FUN on the reference leg (same shifted threshold; asserted as exact Fraction equality of the two independently derived values)
D3-FUN == baseline-FUN (a loss rebate never lifts a losing session above B0 for rho <= 1; asserted exactly)
D1 wash-qualifier EV = (sigma - t)*B0 EXACTLY (100 tickets always staked and always qualifying); the sigma = t pump knife-edge
D2 cap-chaser EV = (sigma - t)*F*E[N] by Wald (every ticket rebated, N <= 200): sign EXACT = sign(sigma - t), = 0 EXACTLY at sigma = t
D1/D2 casual-R1 E[session net incl. comp] = (sigma - t)*B0 EXACTLY (N = 100 fixed, qualification certain, cap not binding)
D3 casual-R1 E[session net incl. comp] = sum_s P(s)*(s + rho*max(-s, 0)) EXACTLY from the same final-net distributions
t=0 control: T1xR1 grinder uncapped P_double = 1/3 exactly (optional stopping; both parents' control)
E[prize] = (1-t)*F exactly in Fractions per (shape, t)
arm_a.ruin_T1xR1_baseline: V025's three derivations reused verbatim: gambler's-ruin closed form + independent exact-Fraction tridiagonal elimination + exact finite-horizon capped DP (4,000 rounds, 151 states) for t in {0, 0.05, 0.1}; must equal V025's committed values (identity_gate below)
arm_a.wipe_R1: exact casual R1 wipe from the same distributions: baseline s <= -90; D1/D2 s <= -90 - 100*sigma (= 0 exactly for sigma = 0.2 on T1: below the support); D3 wipe = 0 EXACTLY for every rho >= 0.2 (wealth at total ruin = rho*B0 >= 0.2*B0 > 0.1*B0 - 'cashback buys SAFE')

boundaries_restated: bankroll-RELATIVE: chips normalized to B0; nothing here prices the casino sink against fishing/mining faucet mint in absolute chips/hr - that needs the live earn-rate baseline whose absence V001/V008 named and V022/V025 restated; the telemetry caveat applies verbatim and is restated, not re-attempted
i.i.d. tickets: no skill edge; a game's strategy spread folds into the take as the REALIZED take
PER-SESSION comp accounting: cross-session comp banking is out of scope; the multi-session mint rate is bounded linearly by the per-session farmer EV the MINT band caps (stated)

finite policy + farmer sets: R1/R5/RG/MC plus wash-qualifier/cap-chaser/stopper-grid bracket unit, bounded, greedy, loss-chasing, wash-qualifying, cap-chasing and optimal-stopping behavior; the D3 stopper grid is 12 variants, NOT an optimum proof - its max EV is a LOWER bound on the exploit, so a MINT failure stands and a MINT pass at the D3 grid boundary is marked (stated, not proven)

inherited bands: FUN >= 0.25 / SAFE <= 0.05 / SINK <= 0.10 are the parents' pre-registered judgments held BY DESIGN for cell-addressable comparison; MINT <= 0 is this grid's registered faucet line; full curves ship so a re-drawn line re-reads, never re-runs

T3 excluded by citation, not re-run: V025 measured its SINK wall 0.148-0.346 and SAFE 0.275-1.000 across ALL (t, c), and comp weakly RAISES P_double - a lever that only adds player value cannot rescue a shape whose SINK already fails without it; monotonicity spot-asserted on one reporting cell
reporting-only legs that cannot flip the decision: t=0 control, compulsive harm context, T3 monotonicity spot cell, house-net table, aggregated-draw spot check, chained anchors

chained_anchors_reporting_only.anchor_1_surviving_cell: V025's one surviving cell T1 (t=0.05, c=5): FUN exact 0.2737540221256319, worst-policy P_wipe 0.0024 (RG 12/5000), worst-policy P_double 0.0000 (0/2000) / uncapped exact 4.472052208172175e-05, R1 grinder cap_frac 0.0035 - my sigma=0 baseline block must reproduce every row
chained_anchors_reporting_only.anchor_2_C2_near_miss: V025's C2 cell T2 (t=0.05, c=5): worst-policy P_wipe 0.0608 (RG 304/5000) vs the 0.05 band, SINK/FUN passing
chained_anchors_reporting_only.anchor_3_t0_control: T1xR1 grinder uncapped P_double = 1/3 exactly + the T1/T2 FUN identity gate above
chained_anchors_reporting_only.anchor_4_house_net_price_tag: the incumbent (T1, t=0.05, c=5, no comp) expected session cost: casual R1 5.2% of B0 (V025 expected_loss_frac 0.05155), worst-policy 25.2% (RG 0.2518) - every all-pass cell's house-net is read against this row
chained_anchors_reporting_only.note: pre-registered cross-verdict anchors; reporting-only, cannot flip the decision, but implemented as self-checks: exact quantities by string equality, measured quantities at 4x two-sample SE with a 0.4 pp floor (disclosure 15); parent values quoted verbatim from V025's committed results.json @ 5e356ed

constants.agreement_gate_EV: EV quantities (not probabilities) gate at 4x the predicted per-point SE, disclosed per point
constants.arithmetic_note: all walks computed in exact integer CENTI-UNITS (cu): 1 cu = F/100 = 0.1 chip; B0 = 10000 cu, F = 100 cu, prizes are multiples of 100 cu. Every comp amount in the grid is an exact integer in cu: D2 rebate sigma*F in {2, 5, 10, 20} cu/ticket; D1 stipend sigma*B0 in {200, 500, 1000, 2000} cu; D2 cap 2*sigma*B0 in {400, 1000, 2000, 4000} cu (= exactly 200 rebated tickets); D3 rebate rho*(10000 - final_bank) with rho = k/5 and final_bank a multiple of 100 cu (D3 credits nothing in-session) is an exact integer. No floats anywhere in bankroll state.

bands.FUN: reference-leg (R1 casual) P_ahead incl. comp >= 0.25 (= 1/4, exact Fraction comparison; Arm A analytic for ALL cells)
bands.MINT: max over the 8 swept policy legs (casual + grinder x R1/R5/RG/MC) AND the design's farmer family of E[session net incl. comp] <= 0 (weak inequality); exact-where-covered per disclosure 3; boundary cells (max = 0 exactly, the sigma = t knife-edge) marked
bands.SAFE: max P_wipe incl. comp over the 4 swept policies (casual) <= 0.05 (integer cross-multiplication on counts: 20*wipes <= M)
bands.SINK: max P_double incl. comp over the 4 swept policies (grinder) <= 0.10 (10*doubles <= M; measured P_double is a LOWER bound under the round cap, so SINK failures stand)
bands.provenance: FUN/SAFE/SINK inherited verbatim from V022/V025 BY DESIGN (the inherited-bands contract); MINT is this grid's new pre-registered faucet guard

constants.cap_note: c = 5 FIXED by pre-registration: both parents measured the tight cap load-bearing (V022's MAXBET <= 0.05*B0 rider; V025's 'c=5 is load-bearing' with RG wiping 0.116-1.000 of sessions at c >= 25). No cap axis in this grid.

designs.D1_qualified_stipend: S = sigma*B0 credited at session END iff session handle >= B0 (handle = tickets_staked * F, i.e. tickets >= 100; weak >=)
designs.D2_handle_rebate: sigma*F credited to bankroll PER TICKET at stake time (spendable in-session), stopping once total rebate reaches the per-session cap 2*sigma*B0 (exactly 200 rebated tickets); no end-of-session component
designs.D3_loss_rebate: rho*(B0 - final_bank)+ credited at session END; nothing in-session

farmers.D1_wash_qualifier: R1 for exactly 100 rounds (= the minimum qualifying handle), stop, collect the stipend; M = 5000 primary / 2500 stability (casual-class leg)
farmers.D2_cap_chaser: R1 until total rebate reaches the cap 2*sigma*B0 (= 200 tickets) or 2,000 rounds or bank < F, stop; M = 2000 primary / 1000 stability
farmers.D3_stopper_grid: b in {1, 5} x target tau in {1.1, 1.25, 1.5}*B0 x floor phi in {0, 0.5*B0}, 12 variants: play b'=min(b, bankroll_units) per round, stop-and-collect when bank >= tau or bank <= phi (phi=0: when bank < F); max EV over the grid taken per rho; M = 4000 primary / 2000 stability per variant; the walk is rho-INVARIANT (thresholds on bankroll, comp only at exit) - see disclosure 4
farmers.farmer_M_note: farmer-leg M values are intake-time pins (the proposal pins M only for casual/grinder/compulsive)

policies.MC: chase b = min(2^min(L,7), 5, bankroll_units), L = consecutive net-losing rounds (prizes_cu < stake_cu, rebate excluded), reset on a net win, unchanged on net zero
policies.R5: b = min(5, bankroll_units) (= min(5, c, floor(bank/F)) at c = 5)
policies.RG: greedy b = max affordable = min(c, bankroll_units) = min(5, bankroll_units) - STRUCTURALLY IDENTICAL to R5 at c = 5 (kept as a separate swept leg with independent draws per the verbatim V025 policy list; the identity is disclosed, not exploited)

constants.prize_scaling: level probability at take t = (1-t) * base_prob; asserted in exact Fractions at start: E[prize] = (1-t)*F exactly per (shape, t)

T3_reporting_only.note: T3 exists ONLY for the seed-20260754 monotonicity spot cell; excluded from every decision number by citation of V025's measured walls

profiles.casual: 100 rounds, stopping early only when bankroll < F (bank < 100 cu); metrics per (cell, policy): P_ahead = P(final wealth incl. comp > B0), P_wipe = P(final wealth incl. comp <= 0.1*B0), E[session net incl. comp]
profiles.compulsive: reporting-only, R1, to ruin (bank < F) or 20,000 rounds; rides the seed-20260754 reporting stream
profiles.grinder: play to WEALTH-INCLUDING-COMP >= 2*B0 (double) or <= 0.5*B0 (half), hard cap 4,000 rounds; wealth = bank (baseline, D2 - rebate already in bank) / bank + stipend*[tickets >= 100] (D1) / bank + rho*(B0 - bank)+ (D3) - the proposal's 'shifted stop targets', checked after every round; a session whose bank drops below F with wealth strictly between the bands ends STRANDED (reachable only under D3 rho >= 0.6, where the wealth floor is unreachable); > 1% cap-hits marks the cell indeterminate

constants.python: cpython-3.11 (minor version pinned in results.json and asserted at start)
constants.seed_note: seeds 20260752-55 allocated strictly above the PROPOSAL 026 registry high-water 20260751
constants.stability_leg: half M everywhere (2500/1000/2500/1000/2000), seed 20260753, identical pinned loop order; must reproduce the same ruling
constants.t_excluded_note: t <= 0.02 excluded by V025's own indeterminacy measurement (grinder cap_frac 0.17-0.32); t=0 control rides the seed-20260754 reporting stream, never a decision number
constants.ticket_resolution: a round's b tickets are b INDEPENDENT draws, exactly one rng.random() per ticket, consumed in ticket order; per ticket, prize = level j prize iff cum_{j-1} <= u < cum_j on the (1-t)-scaled cumulative thresholds

gate_calibration_disclosure.handling_protocol: every gate point reports value, exact, deviation, predicted SE and z-score; every point breaching 1.0 pp is re-measured at 8x pooled M on the aux stream (seed 20260755; predicted SE shrinks ~2.8x, making 1.0 pp >= 4.5 sigma on every point class) and MUST land within the same 1.0 pp tolerance - any 8x re-measure failure fails the run (exit 1). Breaches + re-measures are fully disclosed in results.json. Decision impact: NONE possible within this protocol - every gated quantity's decision reading is its Arm-A exact value (FUN, wipe, exact MINT legs, stopper EVs), so an in-protocol sampling breach cannot move a band edge; the gates condition the validity of the MC arm, whose decision role is confined to the MC-only SAFE/SINK/MINT legs that have no Arm-A counterpart and hence no gate.
gate_calibration_disclosure.pooling: probability gate points pool primary + stability legs of every leg measuring the SAME exact quantity: sigma-shifted FUN/wipe points pool the D1 and D2 legs at that (shape, t, sigma) (M = 2*(5000+2500) = 15,000); baseline FUN/wipe points pool the baseline leg + all four D3 legs (FUN/wipe identical by the exact D3 identities; M = 5*(5000+2500) = 37,500); T1 baseline grinder P_double pools primary + stability (M = 3,000); D3 T1 stopper P(tau) points pool primary + stability (M = 6,000)

predicted_SE_pp.sigma_shifted_FUN_points: sqrt(p*(1-p)/15000)*100 <= 0.41 pp at p = 0.5 -> the 1.0 pp gate is a >= 2.4 sigma gate; ~16 such points: P(at least one breach) is non-trivial (~10-20%)
predicted_SE_pp.stopper_P_tau_points: p up to ~0.37 -> SE up to 0.62 pp -> the 1.0 pp gate is a ~1.6 sigma gate on the mid-p variants; ~24 points: BREACHES ARE EXPECTED BY CONSTRUCTION (a registration-calibration artifact of the same class V027 disclosed)

gate_calibration_disclosure.written: BEFORE any run (fixtures-before-runner discipline); the handling protocol is pinned here so a breach discovered later cannot be outcome-motivated

hand_derived_pins.derivation: RG bets b = min(5, bank//100), start 10000 cu; rounds 1-24: b = 5, stake 500, prizes 0, rebate 5*20 = 100 -> bank -= 400 per round; after round 24 bank = 10000 - 24*400 = 400. Round 25: bank 400 affords only b = 4 (the registered b <= floor(bank/F) rule) -> stake 400, rebate 80 -> bank = 80 < 100 -> casual early stop. tickets = 24*5 + 4 = 124, rebate_total = 2480 < cap 4000. Final wealth = 80 (D2 has no end comp) -> wipe (<= 1000), not ahead. Conservation: 10000 + 0 - 12400 + 2480 = 80.
hand_derived_pins.derivation_correction: DISCLOSED CORRECTION: the originally committed derivation asserted b = 5 on round 25 and tickets = 125, mis-applying the affordability clamp on the final round (bank = 400 cu affords 4 tickets, not 5). The first full run failed exactly this pin (kernel and twin both returned 124/2480/80, agreeing with the registered b <= floor(bankroll/F) rule); the EXPECTATION was corrected to the hand-recomputed values above before the accepted runs. No decision constant, band, seed, M, or convention changed - the error was in the pin's arithmetic, not the model, and the correction is disclosed here, in the session card, and in REPORT.md.
hand_derived_pins.name: pin-1: D2 rebate crediting + casual early-death (T1, t=0.05, D2 sigma=0.2, policy RG, casual)

hand_derived_pins.derivation: R1: win round net = -100 + 200 + 20 = +120 cu; loss round net = -100 + 0 + 20 = -80 cu. After 200 rounds (100 wins, 100 losses): bank = 10000 + 100*120 - 100*80 = 14000 cu; tickets = 200 -> rebate_total = 200*20 = 4000 = cap EXACTLY -> farmer stops on the cap condition (before the 2,000-round horizon). Session net = +4000 cu = +0.4*B0 = 2*(sigma - t)*B0 with sigma=0.2, t=0.05 on this crafted path.
hand_derived_pins.name: pin-2: D2 cap-chaser stops EXACTLY at the rebate cap (T1, t=0.05, D2 sigma=0.2, farmer cap-chaser)

hand_derived_pins.derivation: R5 bets min(5, bank//100): rounds 1-20 bet 5 and lose -> bank 10000 - 500k; after round 20 bank = 0 -> stop. tickets = 100 -> handle = B0 EXACTLY -> qualifies (weak >=) -> stipend 500 cu. Final wealth = 0 + 500 = 500 <= 1000 -> STILL a wipe; not ahead.
hand_derived_pins.name: pin-3: D1 qualification at EXACTLY 100 tickets + wipe WITH stipend (T1, t=0.05, D1 sigma=0.05, policy R5, casual)

hand_derived_pins.derivation: each loss: bank -= 100 cu. Wealth after k losses = (10000 - 100k) + (4/5)*(100k) = 10000 - 20k cu; the half-stop needs wealth <= 5000 -> k >= 250: UNREACHABLE. At k = 100: bank = 0 < 100 -> cannot stake -> session ends STRANDED with wealth = 8000 cu (= rho*B0), rounds = 100; not a double, not a half, not a cap-hit.
hand_derived_pins.name: pin-4: D3 STRANDED grinder (T1, t=0.10, D3 rho=0.8, policy R1, grinder)

hand_derived_pins.derivation: rounds 1-19: 5 wins each, net +500 cu -> bank = 10000 + 19*500 = 19500 (tickets 95; wealth 19500 < 20000: no stop, not yet qualified). Round 20: 1 win + 4 losses, net = 200 - 500 = -300 -> bank = 19200, tickets = 100 -> QUALIFIED -> wealth = 19200 + 2000 = 21200 >= 20000 -> DOUBLE by the stipend jump alone (bank never reached 2*B0). prizes = 96*200 = 19200 cu; conservation: 10000 + 19200 - 10000 = 19200.
hand_derived_pins.name: pin-5: D1 stipend jump CROSSES the double line at qualification (T1, t=0.05, D1 sigma=0.2, policy R5, grinder)

honest_null: a NULL lands as a finalized verdict naming the binding axis (per-design signature table + per-axis pass shares), not a re-run request

identity_gate.statement: cross-verdict identity, pre-registered: the sigma=0 baseline T1 FUN exact Fractions at t in {0, 0.05, 0.10} MUST equal V022's committed G1 reference values (quoted below verbatim from sims/verdict-025-ticket-envelope/fixtures.json identity_gate @ 5e356ed, itself quoting V022 @ fda94d0) by EXACT rational equality - the grid's third chained verdict on one binomial; the T2 baseline Fractions must equal V025's committed strings the same way

intake_time_decisions_disclosed: 1. REPORTING-STREAM ASSIGNMENT: the t=0 control and ALL compulsive legs ride the seed-20260754 reporting stream (the proposal's explicit seed assignment 'reporting legs seed 20260754 - t=0 control, compulsive, the house-net table, the T3 monotonicity spot cell, and the per-round aggregated-draw spot check' is more specific than the loop-order clause's 'casual -> grinder -> compulsive' profile listing and therefore wins); the primary/stability decision streams carry casual + grinder only.

2. BOUNDARY RULE (the sigma = t mint knife-edge, pinned BEFORE any run): a cell whose MINT maximum is 0 EXACTLY (reachable only through the exact-arm legs - D1 wash/casual-R1 and D2 cap-chaser/casual-R1 at sigma = t) PASSES the band as registered (weak inequality) and is marked BOUNDARY; boundary-marked cells cannot be members of an APPROVE consecutive-size band and cannot count as 'clean' rescues ('a band, not a knife-edge'; the NULL clause's own 'MINT-boundary-only passes'); they DO block REJECT (a boundary rescue is not a measured-dead lever - it lands NULL). Identical conservative treatment to indeterminate cells ('route to NULL, never to APPROVE').

3. MINT EXACT-WHERE-COVERED: every MINT leg with an Arm-A exact EV (casual-R1 all designs; D1 wash farmer; D2 cap-chaser at sigma = t exact zero, elsewhere sign-exact with the MC integer total as the reported value; D3 T1 stopper EVs) is DECIDED by the exact value (Fraction comparison); MC-only legs (casual R5/RG/MC, all grinder legs, D3 T2 stopper) are decided by exact integer session-net totals (sum of (wealth_cu - 10000) over replications <= 0). This keeps the sigma = t knife-edge deterministic instead of a coin flip on MC noise, exactly as the proposal's 'EV = 0 exactly ... marked boundary' requires.

4. D3 STOPPER RHO-INVARIANCE: the stopper walk stops on BANKROLL thresholds (tau, phi) and rho enters only through the exit-time comp, so the 12 variant walks are run ONCE per (shape, t) at M_stopper and each rho's EV derives from the same exit records - draw-for-draw equivalent to running them per rho, 4x cheaper, and the pinned loop order stays unambiguous (stopper block after the D3 size loop).

5. FARMER M PINS: wash 5,000 (casual-class: fixed 100 rounds), cap-chaser 2,000, stopper 4,000 per variant (the proposal pins M only for casual/grinder/compulsive; these are intake-time pins, halved in the stability leg).

6. STOPPER TERMINATION GUARD: the stopper chains absorb almost surely (negative drift at every decision t); the MC carries a 200,000-round guard that is a SELF-CHECK sentinel (expected hits: 0; any hit fails the run), not a censoring convention.

7. COMPULSIVE DESIGN COVERAGE: end-of-session comp (D1 stipend, D3 rebate) cannot alter an in-session walk, so compulsive under D1/D3 is DRAW-IDENTICAL to the baseline compulsive leg (an identity, not an approximation): compulsive runs on the 4 baseline cells (+ t=0 control) and the 16 D2 cells (where the continuous rebate genuinely changes ruin timing); D1/D3 cells report the baseline row by identity, labeled as such.

8. GRINDER OUTCOME CLASSES: double (wealth >= 2*B0) / half (wealth <= 0.5*B0) / STRANDED (bank < F with wealth strictly between the bands - reachable only under D3 rho >= 0.6 where the wealth floor sits below the total-ruin wealth rho*B0) / cap-hit (4,000 rounds). Stranded is a COMPLETED, determinate session counted in the denominator and never a double; only cap-hits > 1% mark the cell indeterminate.

9. MC-CHASE STREAK CONVENTION (V025 disclosures 3-4 transposed): L compares prizes_cu to stake_cu, REBATE EXCLUDED (the rebate is comp, not a prize); net-zero rounds leave L unchanged; exponent clip b = min(2^min(L,7), 5, units) is stake-identical to min(2^L, .) at c = 5.

10. CASUAL STOP: 'bankroll < F' = bank < 100 cu (D2's rebate-augmented bankroll included); an R1-casual session can never stop early under ANY design (minimum per-round net -100 + 0 cu from start 10000 cu over 100 rounds; D2 only adds), so the R1 casual leg is always the exact 100-step binomial/DP object Arm A computes - the FUN/wipe/E[net] reference identities hold with certainty, not approximately.

11. RG == R5 AT c = 5: greedy b = min(c, units) = min(5, units) = R5's rule; both legs are kept and swept independently per the proposal's verbatim policy list (their agreement is itself a free consistency signal; disclosed so no reader mistakes them for independent behavioral evidence).
12. GATE-CALIBRATION DISCLOSURE (SE arithmetic done BEFORE the run - the V027 lesson, applied at registration): see gate_calibration_disclosure.

13. EV AGREEMENT GATES: EV quantities gate at 4x predicted SE (an EV is not a probability; the proposal's 1.0 pp gate applies to the probability quantities); predicted SEs computed from the exact per-session variance where Arm A knows it, else from the measured sample variance, disclosed per point.

14. SCRATCHPAD PROBE DISCLOSURE: a pre-commit scratchpad feasibility probe (the comp-shifted FUN table, the exact D3 casual-R1 EVs, one stopper-DP timing run) ran before this fixtures commit; every decision constant was already pinned by the idea file and every convention in this file was written before the probe's numbers were read, so no free parameter remained tunable.

15. ANCHOR TOLERANCE: baseline-reproduction anchors compare this sim's fresh-seed MC counts to V025's quoted measured rows at 4x two-sample binomial SE (sqrt(p*(1-p)*(1/M_mine + 1/M_parent))) with an absolute floor of 0.004 (guards the zero-count cells); exact quantities (Fractions, capped-DP floats from the identical algorithm) must be EQUAL. Anchors are reporting-only for the decision but run as hard self-checks.

16. REBATE TIMING: D2's per-ticket rebate is credited in the same round-end bank update as prizes and stake (bank += prizes + rebate - stake); within-round ordering is unobservable (no stop condition is evaluated mid-round) and the cap accounting is per-ticket exact (cap/rebate = exactly 200 tickets, integer cu).

17. HOUSE-NET TABLE: pure arithmetic over the primary legs' measured integer session-net totals and the Arm-A exact values (house net per session = -E[player session net incl. comp]); no fresh draws - listed under the seed-20260754 reporting umbrella per the proposal, sourced from the committed primary tables.

18. STABILITY-LEG SCOPE: the stability ruling re-evaluates every MC-decided quantity at half M under seed 20260753; Arm-A exact values (FUN, exact MINT legs, boundary marks) are seedless constants shared by construction - what stability tests is every MC-decided band edge and the ruling's count structure.

decision_rule_evaluated_in_this_order: REJECT ('the third lever falls - the triage is complete') iff NO (design, size) rescues C1 AND none rescues C2, in both arms where covered, counting NEITHER determinate all-four-pass cells NOR boundary-marked passes NOR indeterminate all-four-band cells as existing at either target (if the only 'rescues' are boundary or indeterminate, REJECT does NOT fire - those land NULL per the proposal's own NULL clause)

APPROVE ('comp completes the casino') iff some design D* rescues C1 with >= 2 CONSECUTIVE sizes all-four-pass where every member cell is determinate AND non-boundary ('a band, not a knife-edge'), reproduced by the stability leg - house-net table attached

NULL (anything else): C2-only rescues, single-size knife-edges, MINT-boundary-only passes, or indeterminate cells in the passing set; the per-design signature table IS the pin, flip axis named via per-axis pass shares
process.indeterminacy_soundness: failure is always definitive, passes are what indeterminacy can spoil: FUN is analytic (exact); SAFE sessions are casual (always complete); measured P_double under the cap is a lower bound so SINK failures stand; indeterminate cells route to NULL, never to APPROVE
process.loop_order: ONE random.Random(seed) stream per arm-leg flows in this pinned order: shape T1 < T2; t ascending [0.05, 0.1]; within (shape, t): the sigma=0 BASELINE block first (policies R1/R5/RG/MC, each casual M_casual then grinder M_grinder), then design D1 (sizes sigma ascending; per size: policies R1/R5/RG/MC each casual-then-grinder, then the D1 wash-qualifier farmer leg), then design D2 (same, with the D2 cap-chaser farmer leg per size), then design D3 (sizes rho ascending, policies only - the stopper walk is rho-invariant), then the D3 stopper block: 12 variants in pinned order (b ascending, then tau ascending, then phi ascending), M_stopper walks each. Replications sequential, one rng.random() per ticket, no other stream consumption.
process.round_step: per round: compute b from policy state; draw b tickets (one uniform each, ticket order); bank += prizes_cu + rebate_cu - 100*b; update MC streak on prizes vs stake; increment round; re-check stop conditions

target_cells.C1: (T1, t=0.10) - the FUN-dead sink-firm take; baseline FUN 0.13457621... (V022's committed G1 e=0.10 value, V025-identical binomial)
target_cells.C2: (T2, t=0.05) - the SAFE near-miss; V025's worst-policy P_wipe 0.0608 vs the 0.05 band, binding policy RG, its SINK/FUN passing
target_cells.rescued: all four bands (FUN and SAFE and SINK and MINT) pass at that (shape, take) for some (design, size), determinate cells only

process.twin_evaluators: two independently coded decision evaluators (integer cross-multiplication on counts; Fraction comparisons) must agree on every band, every cell, and the ruling

question: Under the pinned comp-envelope model on VERDICT 025's surviving ticket substrate (B0 = 1,000 integer chips; F = 0.01*B0 = 10; per-round ticket cap c = 5 FIXED; prize shapes T1 double-up {2F w.p. (1-t)/2} and T2 tiered {8F, 3F, 1F w.p. (1-t)*{0.05, 0.10, 0.30}} with E[prize] = (1-t)*F exact and T3 jackpot EXCLUDED BY CITATION (V025's measured SINK wall 0.148-0.346 and SAFE 0.275-1.000 across all (t, c)) plus comp monotonicity spot-asserted on one T3 reporting cell; takes t in {0.05, 0.10} + t=0 control reporting-only; comp designs D1 QUALIFIED STIPEND S = sigma*B0 at session end iff session handle >= B0, D2 HANDLE REBATE sigma*F per ticket credited to bankroll continuously with per-session rebate cap 2*sigma*B0, D3 LOSS REBATE rho*(B0 - final)+ at session end - D1/D2 over sigma in {0.02, 0.05, 0.10, 0.20}, D3 over rho in {0.2, 0.4, 0.6, 0.8}; 48 decision cells = 2 shapes x 2 takes x 3 designs x 4 sizes, plus 4 sigma=0 baseline cells that must REPRODUCE V025's committed rows; re-buy policies R1/R5/RG/MC and profiles CASUAL/GRINDER/COMPULSIVE verbatim from V025 with comp counted in final wealth; FARMER family per design - D1 wash-qualifier, D2 cap-chaser, D3 12-variant stopper grid, max EV taken), for which (design, size) cells do FUN (reference-leg R1 P_ahead incl. comp >= 0.25), SAFE (max-policy P_wipe incl. comp <= 0.05), SINK (max-policy P_double incl. comp <= 0.10) and MINT (max over the 4 swept policies AND the farmer family of E[session net incl. comp] <= 0, weak inequality, boundary cells marked) hold simultaneously, determinate cells only - and do any RESCUE the pre-registered target cells C1 = (T1, t=0.10) (the FUN-dead sink-firm take, baseline FUN 0.13457621...) and C2 = (T2, t=0.05) (the SAFE near-miss, V025's worst-policy P_wipe 0.0608, binding policy RG) - landing (evaluated in this pre-registered order) REJECT (no cell rescues C1 AND none rescues C2, both arms where covered), APPROVE (some design rescues C1 with >= 2 CONSECUTIVE sizes all-four-pass, a band not a knife-edge, stability-reproduced, house-net table attached), or NULL (anything else - the per-design signature table IS the pin, flip axis named via per-axis pass shares)?

reporting_legs_seed_20260754.cannot_flip: all four legs are registered as unable to flip the decision
reporting_legs_seed_20260754.order: pinned: (1) t=0 control - T1 casual R1 M=5000, T1 grinder R1 M=2000, T2 casual R1 M=5000, gated against the exact DP/capped-DP values and the Wald t=0 prize-fairness form; (2) compulsive block - for shape in [T1, T2], t in [0.05, 0.1]: baseline R1 M=500, then D2 sigma ascending M=500 each; (3) T3 monotonicity spot cell - T3 (t=0.05) baseline grinder R1 M=2000 then D1 sigma=0.2 grinder R1 M=2000, asserting P_double(D1 sigma=0.2) >= P_double(baseline) (the proposal's 'comp weakly raises P_double', expected margin >> MC noise: the stipend moves the double line from 200 to 180 units); (4) the aggregated-draw spot check - V025's clause transposed verbatim: T2 (t=0.05), 1,000 rounds of 100 tickets, per-ticket draws vs binomial-inversion aggregation, means within 4 SE (reporting-only; every decision number in this sim uses per-ticket draws)
